title: Release candidate, version, installer, and upgrade audit

acceptance_criteria.text: Cut a release-candidate checklist for the next desktop release, including version source of truth, changelog cut, tag plan, installer artifact, checksum artifact, and rollback note.
evidence: docs/release-candidate-1.4.0.md records version source of truth, release scope, publication plan, rollback plan, signing status, and required gates.

acceptance_criteria.text: Resolve the current stable/latest-release documentation mismatch so README, CHANGELOG, package version, Tauri version, VERSION, and release artifacts tell one consistent story.
evidence: VERSION corrected to 1.4.0; package.json, src-tauri/tauri.conf.json, and VERSION now align.
README and CHANGELOG describe 1.4.0 as the current local release candidate, with GitHub publication pending external billing resolution.

acceptance_criteria.text: Verify installer upgrade behavior from the old Apex A+ naming line and current SkillForge line, documenting whether installs replace, coexist, or migrate.
evidence: Silent smoke installed Apex A+ Academy 1.2.1, confirmed Apex uninstall entry, then installed SkillForge Academy 1.4.0.
After SkillForge install, Apex HKLM/HKCU uninstall entries were absent, SkillForge registry version was 1.4.0, and %APPDATA%/com.apexlearning.aplusacademy still existed.
README upgrade wording corrected to say the pre-install hook removes the old Apex installation while preserving shared app data.

acceptance_criteria.text: Run a Windows release smoke test: install, launch, switch tracks, answer content, create backup, close/reopen, uninstall/reinstall or upgrade, and confirm learner data behavior.
evidence: Noninteractive release smoke covered installed app launch, installer reinstall/upgrade path, legacy Apex removal, and app-data directory preservation.
npm test -- src/backup.test.ts --run passed 4 backup-specific tests.

Interactive manual UI smoke for track switching, answering content, and creating a backup was not performed in this noninteractive pass; deeper backup and real-learner workflow validation are tracked by 221 and 228.

acceptance_criteria.text: Record a release audit with commands, installer paths, checksum evidence, signing status, SmartScreen expectation, and residual risks.

source: Gap review: release/version cleanup and upgrade/install behavior audit
